title: Cybersecurity Awareness Education
verbatim: A financial institution must provide adequate and regular technology and cybersecurity awareness education for all staff in undertaking their respective roles and measure the effectiveness of its education and awareness programs. This cybersecurity awareness education must be conducted at least annually and must reflect the evolving cyber threat landscape and emerging risks. Where appropriate, the financial institution shall also include its third party service providers in their relevant training as outlined in paragraph 10.48(i).
translation: All employees, contractors, and third parties with system access must complete mandatory annual security awareness training covering cyber risks, data protection, and your security policies. Track and report on training completion rates.

artifacts.name: Security Awareness Program
artifacts.description: Documented security awareness program covering all personnel with system access

artifacts.name: Training Materials
artifacts.description: Annual cybersecurity awareness training content and materials

artifacts.name: Training Completion Report
artifacts.description: Report tracking training completion rates across all personnel

title: Technology Staff Training
verbatim: A financial institution must provide adequate and continuous training for staff involved in technology operations, cybersecurity and risk management in order to ensure that the staff are competent and suitably certified to effectively perform their roles and responsibilities.
translation: Run phishing simulations at least quarterly to test how your people respond to social engineering. Anyone who falls for the simulation must receive additional training. Track metrics over time and report them to management to measure improvement.
evidence: Follow-up training records for failed simulations
Phishing simulation metrics and trend reports

artifacts.name: Phishing Simulation Procedure
artifacts.description: Procedure for conducting quarterly phishing simulations and follow-up training

artifacts.name: Phishing Simulation Reports
artifacts.description: Quarterly reports on phishing simulation results and failure rates

artifacts.name: Follow-Up Training Records
artifacts.description: Records of follow-up training for personnel who failed phishing simulations

title: Board Technology Training
verbatim: A financial institution must provide its board members with regular training and information on technology developments to enable the board to effectively discharge its oversight role.
translation: Provide specialised security training tailored to specific roles such as IT administrators, developers, security analysts, and executives. Training content should reflect the unique risks and responsibilities of each role and be updated as threats evolve.

artifacts.name: Role-Based Training Program
artifacts.description: Program defining specialised security training requirements by role

artifacts.name: Role-Specific Training Materials
artifacts.description: Training materials tailored to specific roles and responsibilities

artifacts.name: Role-Based Training Completion Report
artifacts.description: Report on role-based training completion and effectiveness
